---
artifact_id: ESE111-CH04-DEMO
pair_id: ESE111-CH04-DEMO
lang: en
pair_revision: 0.1.0
semantic_id: ESE111-CH04-DEMO
id: ESE111-CH04-DEMO-en
resource_type: demonstration
language: en
paired_with: ESE111-CH04-DEMO-fr
version: 0.1.0
lifecycle: drafting
outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-07, PLO-08]
estimated_student_time_min: 10
safety_level: L1
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
technical_contract:
  outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
  equations: [KCL signed current sum equals zero, KVL signed loop voltage sum equals zero, v equals R times i, power equals v times i]
  values: {source_v: 6.0, r1_ohm: 10000, r2_ohm: 22000, r3_ohm: 33000, r4_baseline_ohm: 15000, r4_redesign_ohm: 22000}
  safety_limits: {source_max_v: 6.1, current_limit_max_a: 0.02, dmm_current_input_permitted: false}
  expected_ranges: {baseline_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.087, 1.155], redesign_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.412, 1.500], source_current_simulated_a: [0.000229, 0.000244]}
  rubric_total: 28
  answer_key_digest: public-formative-key-v0.1.0
  figure_ids: [ESE111-CH04-FIG-001]
---

# Demonstration — The probe becomes a branch

## Question and learner decision

“Will TP-B rise, fall, or remain unchanged when a finite-input-resistance voltage
channel is attached?” Learners vote, state a mechanism, and predict the missing
KCL term before any output is revealed. After the result, they choose whether the
network, meter, or model should change first.

## Live setup, only after approval

Use the physically piloted LAB04 baseline, approved source and supervision, a
function-checked reference DMM, and a second qualified 1 MΩ input channel or an
approved equivalent loading resistor that cannot be confused with an instrument
accuracy test. Record source/meter IDs, input resistances, measured R values,
ambient note, setup photo, inspection, actual ranges, and reset time in the staff
pilot record. No current-input use.

With output off: inspect topology, set 6.0 V/$\leq 20$ mA, leads in COM/VΩ, and obtain
checkpoint. Energize without touching. Read TP-B on the reference route; attach
the declared second channel; read both; remove it and confirm recovery. Isolate
before adjustment. Stop on any LAB04 condition.

The analytical nominal pair is 1.1276 V unloaded versus 1.1154 V at 1 MΩ. It is a
screening expectation, not a live-demo claim. Accept the demo only if the measured
change agrees in direction and magnitude with a model using the actual input and
declared uncertainty; otherwise stop and investigate rather than force the story.

## Static and failure fallback

Use the slide's two calculated values, the PREP-A row, and a board drawing of
$R_M$ from B to COM. Learners still calculate $I_M$, add it to KCL-B, and name
a disconfirming test. This route survives absent internet, power interruption,
missing instrument, unstable contact, projector failure (print), and an inaccessible
view (spoken text/table). It teaches analysis but makes no physical competence claim.

Recovery: output off → isolate → verify zero → preserve readings/configuration →
label the failure → continue static or end at the safe checkpoint. Never swap an
unqualified meter live. Record failure, learner impact, owner, and required retest.
